fields.name: activities_guidance
fields.label: What activities has your organisation organised or assisted with that have furthered it's goals or objectives during this reporting period? These events should have had a measurable impact on your organisation or the community members you work with.
fields.notice: How should I answer this question? Please list all the various activities your organisation has undertaken for this reporting period. These can include workshops, training, marches, community meetings, door-to-door campaigns, awareness campaigns, fundraising events etc. If you feel the event you ran does not fit within the groupings provided, please select "Other".

fields.name: casework_intro
fields.label: How many new cases did your organisation handle during this reporting period and how many of these cases were brought in by third-parties?

fields.name: casework_guidance
fields.notice: How should I answer this question? Please provide a count of all new cases broken down by the different categories and case types listed. If possible, please separate your cases into those opened by the client themselves (the person effected directly by the case) and those opened by third parties on behalf of the client (eg. a concerned colleague or family member).

config.headerLabel: New cases opened during this reporting period

fields.name: finalised_intro
fields.label: How many cases did your organisation finalise during this reporting period and what were the demographic details (gender, age and race) of the clients in those cases?

fields.name: finalised_guidance
fields.notice: How should I answer this question? Please provide a count of all finalised cases broken down by the different categories and case types listed. Please separate your cases into those that were referred, and those that were closed in any other manner.

config.casesNotice: Any new categories that are not filled in here, will not appear in any subsequent reporting data. Please fill in this year of the form before proceeding to the next.

fields.name: household_gardens_count
fields.label: New household food gardens you helped establish
fields.description: During this reporting period (April 1, 2025 - July 31, 2025), how many new household food gardens (you must be able to verify that this garden exists) did you support in establishing through direct labour, seeds, equipment or other resources, or training/advice?

fields.name: community_gardens_intro
fields.label: How many community food gardens does your organisation own or manage?

fields.name: community_gardens_guidance
fields.notice: How should I answer this question? The list of gardens that were present in your previous narrative report have been added below. Please check and update the list to ensure all gardens are accounted for and all data is up-to-date and accurate.

fields.name: community_gardens
fields.label: Name and details
fields.subtitle: of each community garden
fields.notice: Please note! Community gardens that are not in this list, will not appear in any subsequent reporting lists. If you delete a garden from this list, it will be deleted from all other reporting lists.

fields.name: beneficiaries_intro
fields.label: How many organisations and individuals benefit from your community gardening?

fields.name: beneficiaries_guidance

Please check and update the list to ensure all employees and beneficiaries are accounted for and all related data is up-to-date and accurate.

config.beneficiariesDescription: A beneficiary is any organisation or group that benefits from the harvested outputs of your community garden.

fields.name: yields_intro
fields.label: During this reporting period (1 April, 2025 - 31 July 2025), what food items did you plant and/or harvest in your community gardens?

fields.name: yields_guidance
fields.notice: How should I answer this question? Where possible, we have populated each farm with the items that were planted or harvest in the last reporting period. Please add or remove items from this list until it reflects accurately. If an item was planted in the last reporting period, please do not include that planted amount in your totals. However, please include any harvested amount in your harvested total.

fields.name: challenges_intro
fields.label: During the last reporting period, across all your activities, operations and in the communities you work in, what existing or new challenges have been preventing growth, prosperity or impact?

fields.name: challenges_guidance
fields.notice: How should I answer this question? We have added the past challenges from your previous narrative reports below. Please provide updates on past challenges or add new challenges that arose during this reporting period.

fields.name: successes_intro
fields.label: During the last reporting period (1 April, 2025 - 31 July, 2025), across all your activities, operations and in the communities you work in, what successes did you have that helped your organisation or you community achieve growth, prosperity or impact?

fields.name: successes_guidance
fields.notice: How should I answer this question? Please add any successes to the list below and describe them in no more than 50 words. If you have a noteworthy success that your organisation is particularly proud of, please rather add it to the "High impact case studies" section, as we would love to know more about these noteworthy successes.

sections.title: SCAT requested case studies (stories)
sections.description: Being able to tell stories (case studies) of highly impactful work being done by the organisations that receive funding and support from SCAT is what enables us to secure new funding and opportunities for the organisations we work with. If you have any stories that align with those SCAT is looking to be able to present to funders, please tell us about them!

fields.name: case_studies_guidance
fields.notice: How should I answer this question? Please examine all the areas that SCAT is requesting case studies for. If a story you have matches that area, please provide it there. If you have a story that does not fit in any of those areas, please add it to the "General case studies" section.
